behavior_summary: Provides the guardsman's small interaction set: a U8P_GUARDMAN::look/examine response, an on-use conversation with a short question menu and branching replies, a cast reaction, and area-triggered coordination when the avatar enters or leaves a nearby trigger region.
U8P_GUARDMAN::look replies with an identity bark that depends on whether the NPC is dead (barks either "dead guardsman" or "guardsman").

U8P_GUARDMAN::enterFastArea/leaveFastArea manage area-specific references (resetting stored refs) and, when appropriate, spawn a short spawned routine (U8P_GUARDMAN::095B) to coordinate nearby guards (orient/move them) after randomized waits.

U8P_GUARDMAN::use implements conversational logic: it blocks conversation for special NPC frames (frame 0x14), sets a global somebodyTalking flag while refusing to speak, otherwise greets the player (randomized), offers a small ask() menu (Who are you? What is your duty? Bye) and replies with branch-specific lines. It also spawns helper/movement methods and sets NPC activity as needed.
U8P_GUARDMAN::cast simply barks a call-to-arms line ('Guardsmen, to arms!') when invoked.
notable_control_flow: Early exits in enterFastArea: function returns without spawning/helper actions if the NPC is dead, if Npc::getMap(self) == 3, or if global[0063 01] (secondexecution) is set.

Conversation blocking in U8P_GUARDMAN::use: if Item::getFrame(self) == 0x14 the guard refuses to speak — sets global[000F 01] (somebodyTalking)=1, spawns a short FREE helper, barks an away message, then clears the flag and returns.

Use() chooses greeting and reply branches via urandom, then constructs a short slist and calls Item::ask to drive the dialog tree; replies may spawn movement/activity methods (METHOD::*) or set Npc::cSetActivity.

enterFastArea starts U8P_GUARDMAN::095B (spawn) which runs timed waits (urandom-based), scans nearby items/NPCs, and for eligible NPCs (Item::isNpc && not Npc::isInCombat) spawns method calls to change facing or move them toward a target.
open_questions: Exact semantics of frame value 0x14: why it blocks conversation and how that frame is set externally.
Precise meaning of map value 3 in Npc::getMap(self) (enterFastArea skips init on map==3) — what world state or zone does it represent?
Details of the spawned METHOD::* and FREE::* helper routines (0BCF, 1090, 091C, 15E7, 1C19, 2A56): their precise actions are inferred (movement/delay/dialog helper) but not canonical.
Which exact reference slots (ref IDs) are reset via resetRef and what gameplay entities they track (patrol points, leader, target)?
Behavior of global[0063 01] (secondexecution): inferred to suppress repeated initialization but the higher-level quest/flow conditions that set it are unknown.

main_function.name: U8P_GUARDMAN::look
main_function.purpose: Handle an examine/U8P_GUARDMAN::look interaction: check whether the guardsman NPC is dead and emit the appropriate short identity bark to the requesting process.
entry_conditions: Event 0: player or other actor looks at this NPC (examine event triggers U8P_GUARDMAN::look(pid, self)).

function_map.name: U8P_GUARDMAN::look
function_map.role: Return a short identity bark ('dead guardsman' or 'guardsman') when examined.

function_map.name: U8P_GUARDMAN::enterFastArea
function_map.role: Triggered on area entry: reset area refs and, when appropriate, spawn a short routine to coordinate nearby guards.

function_map.name: U8P_GUARDMAN::leaveFastArea
function_map.role: Triggered on area exit: clear the same set of stored refs to reset area-specific state.

function_map.name: U8P_GUARDMAN::cast
function_map.role: Handle a cast event by barking a call-to-arms line.

function_map.name: U8P_GUARDMAN::use
function_map.role: Main conversational/use handler: greeting, small question menu, branching replies, activity/movement spawns, and conversation blocking for special frames.

function_map.name: U8P_GUARDMAN::095B
function_map.role: Spawned routine started by enterFastArea that waits, scans nearby NPCs, and issues orientation/movement spawns to coordinate guard reactions.

internal_calls.purpose: Spawn a short helper routine to coordinate nearby guard NPCs after the avatar enters the fast area.

external_calls.purpose: Check whether the guardsman is dead to select between 'dead guardsman' and 'guardsman' bark strings.
Emit the identity string to the viewing process.
Read NPC map id and early-exit if map==3.
Clear multiple stored reference slots on the NPC to reset area-specific state.
Clear the same set of stored refs when leaving the trigger area.
Speak the 'Guardsmen, to arms!' line on cast event.
Check for a special frame (0x14) that blocks conversation.
Save the NPC's facing/direction for later orientation or movement spawns.
Present a short question list to the player and obtain their choice.
Pick randomized greeting/reply variants and randomized delays in spawned routines.
Spawn a small helper or delay when conversation is blocked (used while somebodyTalking is set).
Spawned methods used to move/orient the NPC or change activity in response to dialog choices.
Filter nearby items, only apply guard coordination logic to NPCs.
Skip commanding NPCs that are currently in combat.
Compute direction toward another item/NPC when issuing movement/orientation commands.
Set the NPC's scripted activity during or after conversation.
Short timed wait/delay used during conversation flow.

reads.name: global[0063 01] (secondexecution)
reads.likely_effect: Suppress enterFastArea initialization/spawn when set (prevents repeated coordination).

reads.name: global[0058 01] (devonInRule)
reads.likely_effect: Select alternate dialog branches in use() (changes replies for 'Who are you?' / 'What is your duty?').

writes.name: global[000F 01] (somebodyTalking)
writes.likely_effect: Set to 1 to block overlapping speech/dialog while the guard refuses to speak, then cleared after the refusal sequence.

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: Provides the guardsman's small interaction set: a U8P_GUARDMAN::look/examine response, an on-use conversation with a short question menu and branching replies, a cast reaction, and area-triggered coordination when the avatar enters or leaves a nearby trigger region.
